error.title: Failed to load members

feedback.loadFailed: Failed to load the members list.
feedback.unauthorized: Your trainer session expired or you no longer have access to this view.
feedback.detailsRequiresActiveRelationship: You cannot open member details without an active relationship.

page.title: Trainer relationship
page.subtitle: This screen relies only on the real invitation contract. It shows the public invitation status and lets you respond only when the API actually supports it.
guidance: If the invitation link contains a valid identifier and code, the screen loads the public invitation status generated by Orval.
Accept and reject actions are available only for the pending state supported by the accept/reject endpoints.
No invitation context does not automatically mean no relationship exists — it only means the current API contract does not give this view enough data to confirm it.

invitations.title: Invitations assigned to your account
invitations.subtitle: You have {count} pending trainer invitation. You can accept the relationship or reject the invitation without using the email link.
invitations.itemDescription: This invitation was sent to the email assigned to your account or directly to your athlete profile.
invitations.emptySubtitle: If a trainer has just sent an invitation, refresh this view. The list uses /api/trainee/invitations for the signed-in athlete.

summary.accountMeta: Identity comes from the active user session.
summary.contractMeta: This view only uses invitation / accept / reject endpoints.
summary.stateMeta: The state is derived only from the invitation API response.

actions.label: Available actions
actions.available: You can respond to the invitation
actions.pendingDescription: The API confirms a pending state, so you can accept or reject the relationship now.
actions.defaultDescription: Outside the pending state, this view stays informational and does not guess any extra capabilities.

identity.label: Athlete account
identity.description: This section uses only data available in the current session, without hardcoding trainer details.

scope.label: Source of truth
scope.description: The view does not use training-plan endpoints or any indirect relationship proxy.

contract.title: API contract
contract.primaryLine: Only invitation status and accept / reject endpoints are used.
contract.secondaryLine: If the backend does not expose a separate endpoint for the signed-in athlete's current relationship, this screen does not infer it from training plans or other modules.

items.statusMeta: The status field comes directly from PublicInvitationStatusDto.

items.codeMeta: The code is used only to fetch the public invitation status.
items.routeValue: Page opened from an invitation link

loading.title: Checking relationship status
loading.subtitle: This view is loading only invitation data exposed by the current API contract.

error.title: Failed to read invitation status
error.subtitle: Try again. If the problem keeps happening, the API did not return enough data to show the relationship state.

pending.title: You have a trainer invitation waiting
pending.subtitle: You can accept or reject this relationship now. This screen does not show trainer identity fields because the current DTO does not return them.

accepted.title: The invitation has been accepted
accepted.subtitle: The public invitation status returns an accepted state. The screen does not claim anything beyond what the API actually confirms.

rejected.title: This invitation was already rejected
rejected.subtitle: The invitation is no longer actionable and cannot be handled again from this view.

expired.title: This invitation is no longer active
expired.subtitle: The invitation status indicates that the link expired or was revoked, so there is no action left to take.

empty.title: No invitation data to display
empty.subtitle: This view has no active invitation context or the API did not return a status, so it cannot confirm a trainer-athlete relationship without guessing backend behavior.

feedback.loadFailed: Failed to load invitation or trainer relationship data.
feedback.invitationsEndpointMissing: The backend does not expose /api/trainee/invitations yet. Deploy the latest API branch so the athlete panel can load invitations without the email link.
feedback.acceptFailed: Failed to accept the trainer relationship.
feedback.rejectFailed: Failed to reject the trainer invitation.

accept.title: Accepting the collaboration invitation

success.acceptMessage: Your collaboration with the trainer is now active. You can continue to the relationship view.

fields.forceUpdateHint: Enable this when the app should block usage until the new version is installed.

placeholders.releaseNotes: Describe the main changes in this release

feedback.loadFailed: Failed to load the app version configuration.
feedback.submitFailed: Failed to save the app version configuration.

notes.title: How to use it
notes.description: Both mobile platforms use the same API endpoint and are distinguished by the platform field in the payload. Load the current config first or overwrite it directly with a new Android or iOS release.

hero.title: Train with purpose. Track progress. Coach without chaos.
hero.subtitle: LGYM connects a mobile app for athletes with a web dashboard for coaches. Plans, workouts, history, exercises, and athlete collaboration all live in one organized place.

primary.title: Dashboard / Home
primary.description: Place a mobile app screenshot here

panel.title: Athletes, plans, and reports in one view.

features.title: Not another phone note — a real system for training execution.

plan.title: A plan instead of improvising
plan.description: Build training days, save exercises, and track progress without scattered notes.

history.title: History that explains progress
history.description: Workouts, results, and records create context for the next decision — for athletes and coaches.

collaboration.title: Coach–athlete collaboration
collaboration.description: Invitations, plans, reports, and feedback turn the app into a coaching workspace.

context.title: Training with an ELO ranking and real competition.
context.description: LGYM turns workout logging into a progress system: results build an ELO ranking, records matter, and athletes can compare themselves with others while competing for a better position in future workouts.

elo.title: Train solo, but see how you compare with others
elo.description: Workout history, records, and the ELO ranking create motivation for the next set — they do more than store what already happened.

mobilePlan.title: Every workout can move you up the ranking
mobilePlan.description: Sets, load, and records are not just history — they feed an ELO score that shows progress and lets athletes compete with others.

trainerPlatform.title: The coach dashboard as an operating center
trainerPlatform.description: The landing page must sell the web platform too: athletes, plans, reports, and feedback are real value for coaches.

rows.feedback: Reports, notes, and feedback in one place

exerciseLibrary.title: Competition built around specific exercises
exerciseLibrary.description: The exercise library keeps movements structured, so results, records, and user comparisons share the same context.

coachingWorkflow.title: Fewer messages, more structured work
coachingWorkflow.description: The coach platform presents the business value clearly: managing people, training decisions, and communication.

rows.web: Web is better for operational work
rows.mobile: Mobile remains the athlete’s daily interface

progressTracking.title: Train solo, but see how you compare with others
progressTracking.description: Workout history, records, and the ELO ranking create motivation for the next set — they do more than store what already happened.

athletes.title: An app that guides the workout instead of only recording it.
athletes.description: This is the place for the most important mobile screenshots: home, daily plan, active workout, exercises, and history. The section should show athletes that LGYM is a daily progress tool.

trainers.title: A web platform for working with athletes, plans, and feedback.
trainers.description: This is where you can paste final coach dashboard screenshots. For now, the polished product slots communicate which views should go here.

members.title: Client list and details
members.placeholder: Landscape screenshot: athlete list / client details
members.description: Slot for a screenshot with athlete list, relationship status, and quick access to details.

plans.title: Building training plans
plans.placeholder: Landscape screenshot: plan builder / training days
plans.description: Place for a screenshot of the plan builder, training days, or share-code flow.

reports.title: Feedback and work control
reports.description: Slot for reports, notes, measurements, or athlete work history.

mobile.title: For athletes
mobile.copy: Training plan, exercises, history, and progress always within reach.

web.title: For coaches
web.copy: A more convenient way to manage athletes, plans, and reports from the browser.

platform.title: For product growth
platform.copy: One ecosystem for the mobile app, web dashboard, and future campaigns.

socials.title: Follow LGYM’s growth beyond the app.
socials.description: Instagram and TikTok are the place for quick material: user progress, feature previews, coach dashboard screenshots, and short training formats.

download.title: The landing page is ready — replace slots with screenshots and connect store links.
download.description: The buttons below are prepared as placeholders for the final App Store and Google Play links.

admin.subtitle: Manage the exercise library and add new movements either globally or privately for your administrator account.

user.subtitle: See all available exercises, split the library into global and private items, and quickly find the movements you care about.
